  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>RPA Permission</th><th>Deprecated RPA Permission</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View configured settings for your organization.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Organization Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add or modify settings for your organization.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Organization Management Administration</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Configure Currency and Decimal Sign

To configure the currency and decimal sign to show in RPA Manager modules:

1.  In the **Settings** module, click **Admin Settings**.
2.  Ensure that you are in the **General** tab.
3.  Configure the following values:
    - **Currency**

      The currency symbol that RPA Manager shows in modules and dashboards.

    - **Decimal Sign**

      The decimal sign that RPA Manager uses to show fractional numbers in modules and dashboards.

4.  Click **Save**
---

# Admin Settings Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If you use an RPA account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[**Organization Management** documentation](organizationmanagement-overview).

The **Admin Settings View** of the **Settings** module enables you to do the following:

- Configure the currency and decimal sign to show in RPA Manager modules.
- Reassign the RPA assets of deleted or disabled users.
---

# Reassigning RPA Assets

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If an RPA user or team is deleted or disabled in Access Management, the **Reassign Assets** tab in
the **Admin Settings** view of the **Settings** module shows RPA assets that were assigned to this
user or team.

All users with the **Reassign Users** or **RPA Administrator** permission get an email when an RPA
asset is orphaned because its last assigned user is deleted.

The **Reassign Assets** tab can also show all RPA assets of an existing user.

You can reassign the following RPA assets to another user:

- Credentials
- Process Evaluations
- Process Projects
- Process Board Tasks
- Alert Targets

The following assets are deleted on the deletion of the user:

- Private dashboards
- User API Keys

User Tasks are not shown in the **Reassign Assets** view and can be reassigned via the **Unprocessed
User Tasks** view or via the run configurations of a process.

## Before You Begin

- Ask an organization administrator in Access Management to assign you the required permissions:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>RPA Permission</th><th>Deprecated RPA Permission</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View configured settings for your organization.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Organization Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add or modify settings for your organization.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Organization Management Administration</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reassign RPA Assets.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Reassign Users</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- Ensure that the user to whom you reassign an asset has the required permissions and knowledge to
  handle that asset.

## Reassign One RPA Asset

Reassign one asset to another user:

1.  Open the **Reassign Assets** tab in the **Admin Settings** view of the **Settings** module.
2.  To reassign an asset of an existing user, click the **Unassigned assets** drop-down and select
    the user. You can also search for the userâs name to filter the list.
3.  Click the **Reassign** on the panel of the asset to reassign.
4.  Check and edit the **Reassign Owner** form:
    - In **Assign To**, select a user.
    - Select **Send email to the new owner** to inform the selected user about the reassignment.

5.  Click **Save**.

RPA Manager removes the reassigned asset from the list.

A toast message informs you about the success of the reassignment.

## Reassign Several RPA Assets to One User

Reassign several assets to another user at once:

1.  Open the **Reassign Assets** tab in the **Admin Settings** view of the **Settings** module.
2.  To reassign assets of an existing user, click the **Unassigned assets** drop-down and select the
    user. You can also search for the userâs name to filter the list.
3.  Check the panel of every asset to reassign.
4.  Check and edit the **Reassign Owner** form:
    - In **Assign To**, select a user.
    - Select **Send email to the new owner** to inform the selected user about the reassignment.

5.  Click **Save**.

RPA Manager removes the reassigned asset from the list.

A toast message informs you about the success of the reassignments.

## See Also

- [Access Management](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/)
---

# Connecting to RPA Processes from Salesforce Products

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

RPA Manager requires user authentication to integrate RPA processes into other Salesforce products.
For example, you can use your API key to configure an RPA connector in a MuleSoft Composer flow.

If you use an RPA account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[**User Management** documentation](usermanagement-connect).

For security reasons, you can only see and copy an API key on creation.

> [!TIP] You can now use
> [Connected Apps](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/connected-apps-overview) for a more
> secure and scalable authentication method than User API Keys.

## Before You Begin

- Ask an organization administrator in Access Management to assign you the required permissions:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>RPA Permission</th><th>Deprecated RPA Permission</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>View configured settings for your organization.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>Organization Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create, edit, and delete API keys.</p></td><td><p>RPA Administrator</p></td><td><p>User API Key Create, User API Key Edit, User API Key Delete</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- Users must have an Anypoint Platform account to associate the API key with.
- You must have a secure way to transfer the API Key to the user.
- Users must have a secure place to store their API key.

## Create a User API Key

Create an API key for a user to integrate an RPA process into another Salesforce product. After
expiration, the integrated process instance can no longer connect to RPA Manager from other
Salesforce products. Edit the API key to move the expiration date. For security reasons, RPA Manager
shows the API Key only during creation. Copy the API Key and securely send it to the user. If the
user lose an API Key, create a new one.

To create a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  Complete the form **Create User API Key**:
    - **User**:

      Select a user whose identity and permissions are used to connect from another Salesforce
      products to RPA Manager.

    - **Expiration Date**:

      Select an expiration date for the user API key from the calendar.

4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the window **Key Created**, click **Copy to clipboard**.
6.  Copy the API key to a secure place.
7.  Click **Close**.

Send the user API key to the user in a secure way.

The users can copy their API key into the configuration settings of the Salesforce products into
which they want to integrate the RPA processes.

## Edit a User API Key

Edit a user API key to change its name, description, or expiration date. If you lost access to an
API key, create a new one.

To edit a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Edit** (![pen-to-paper symbol](_images/edit-icon.png)) on the panel of the user API key
    to edit.
3.  Edit the form **Create User API Key**:

    For an explanation of the properties, see [**Create a User API Key**](#form-createuserapikey).

4.  Click **Save**.

## Delete a User API Key

Delete a user API key to revoke the userâs REST API access to RPA Manager. Integrated processes that
use the deleted API key stop working.

To delete a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Remove** (![trash symbol](_images/delete-icon.png)) on the panel of the user API key to
    remove.
3.  Confirm the removal.

## See Also

- [Access Management](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/)
- [Connected Apps](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/connected-apps-overview)
- [Create an Invocable Run Configuration](processautomation-deploy#invocable-configuration)
- [MuleSoft RPA Connector for Composer](../composer/ms_composer_rpa_reference)
- [Salesforce: Use Flows with MuleSoft RPA](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_build_use_flows_with_mulesoft_rpa.htm&type=5)
---

# Settings Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If you use an RPA account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[**Organization Management** documentation](organizationmanagement-overview).

The **Settings** module enables you to do the following:

- Create user API keys for connecting to RPA processes from Salesforce products.
- Configure general settings.
- Reassign the RPA assets of deleted or disabled users.
---

# Assembling User Groups

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

In automation projects, several employees often work together on one task. These users can be
assigned to user groups. User groups can be deployed for processing of user tasks during execution
of a process. User groups marked as center of excellence may approve processes for automation.

If you use an Anypoint account to login to RPA Manager, Anypoint teams replace the RPA Manager user
groups. To function as a center of excellence for approving processes for automation or to complete
user tasks, the teams must have the required RPA permissions. To manage teams, refer to the
[Anypoint Access Management documentation](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/).

In the **User Groups** view in the **User Management** module, you can see all existing user groups.

You can create new user groups, check their usage, edit them, and delete them.

## Before You Begin

- Ask an administrator to assign you the required privileges:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Privilege</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Open the <strong>User Management</strong> module.</p></td><td><p>User Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create, edit, and delete user groups.</p></td><td><p>User Group Create, User Group Edit, User Group Delete</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create a User Group

Create a new user group as defined.

1.  In the **User Groups** view of the **User Management** module, click **Create**.
2.  Complete the form **Create a New User Group**:
    - **Center of excellence**:

      Select the **Center of Excellence** checkbox to enable entry of the user group as a project
      manager for process approvals.

    - **Available/Assigned Users**:

      Click on user names to assign them to or remove them from the user group.

3.  Click **Save**.

## Edit a User Group

Edit a user group to change its function or members.

1.  In the **User Groups** view of the **User Management** module, click **Edit** in the row of the
    user group to edit.
2.  Edit the form **Edit User Group**:
    - **Center of excellence**:

      Select the **Center of Excellence** checkbox to enable entry of the user group as a project
      manager for process approvals.

    - **Available/Assigned Users**:

      Click on user names to assign them to or remove them from the user group.

3.  Click **Save**.

## Delete a User Group

Delete user groups no longer needed. Check their usage and reassign their tasks first.

1.  In the **User Groups** view of the **User Management** module, click the icon **Usage** in the
    row of the user group to delete. Proceed only, if the user group is not needed anymore.
2.  In the **User Groups** view of the **User Management** module, click **Remove** in the row of
    the user group to delete.
3.  Confirm the deletion.

## Designate User Groups as Centers of Excellence

User groups marked as center of excellence may approve processes for automation. You can designate
user groups as centers of excellence on creation or edit this property afterwards.

1.  In the **User Groups** view of the **User Management** module, click **Edit** in the row of the
    user group to edit.
2.  Edit the form **Edit User Group**:
    - **Center of excellence**:

      Select the **Center of Excellence** checkbox to enable entry of the user group as a project
      manager for process approvals.

3.  Click **Save**.
---

# Connecting to RPA Processes from Salesforce Products

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If you use an Anypoint account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[**Settings** documentation](settings-connect).

RPA Manager requires user authentication to integrate RPA processes into other Salesforce products.
For example, you can use your API key to configure an RPA connector in a MuleSoft Composer flow.

For security reasons, you can only see and copy your API key on creation.

## Before You Begin

- Ask an administrator to assign you the required privileges:

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Privilege</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Open the <strong>User Management</strong> module.</p></td><td><p>User Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create, edit, and delete API keys.</p></td><td><p>User API Key Create, User API Key Edit, User API Key Delete</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

- You must have an account in RPA Manager to associate your API key with.
- You must have a secure place accessible to store your API key.

## Create a User API Key

Create an API key for yourself to integrate an RPA process into another Salesforce product. After
expiration, the integrated process instance can no longer connect to RPA Manager from other
Salesforce products. Edit the API key to move the expiration date. For security reasons, RPA Manager
shows the API Key only during creation. Copy your API Key and save it to a secure place. If you lose
an API Key, create a new one.

To create a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  Complete the form **Create User API Key**:
    - **User**:

      Select yourself. Your identity and permissions are used to connect from another Salesforce
      products to RPA Manager.

    - **Expiration Date**:

      Select an expiration date for the user API key from the calendar.

4.  Click **OK**.
5.  In the window **Key Created!**, click **Copy to clipboard**.
6.  Copy the API key to a secure place.
7.  Click **Close**.

Copy your saved API key into the configuration settings of the Salesforce products into which you
want to integrate the RPA processes.

## Edit a User API Key

Edit a user API key to change its name, description, or expiration date. If you lost access to your
API key, create a new one.

To edit a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Edit** (![pen-to-paper symbol](_images/edit-icon.png)) on the panel of the user API key
    to edit.
3.  Edit the form **Create User API Key**:

    For an explanation of the properties, see [**Create a User API Key**](#form-createuserapikey).

4.  Click **Save**.

## Delete a User API Key

Delete a user API key to revoke the userâs REST API access to RPA Manager. Integrated processes that
use the deleted API key stop working.

To delete a user API key:

1.  Open the **User API Keys** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Remove** (![trash symbol](_images/delete-icon.png)) on the panel of the user API key to
    remove.
3.  Confirm the removal.

## See Also

- [Create an Invocable Run Configuration](processautomation-deploy#invocable-configuration)
- [MuleSoft RPA Connector for Composer](../composer/ms_composer_rpa_reference)
- [Salesforce: Use Flows with MuleSoft RPA](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.flow_build_use_flows_with_mulesoft_rpa.htm&type=5)
---

# Managing Users

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If you use an Anypoint account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[Anypoint Access Management documentation](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/).

> [!IMPORTANT] If you are your organizationâs first admin user, you are responsible for creating all
> other users. You can do this yourself or create another user with admin privileges to whom you
> delegate this task.

Manage the users of RPA Manager, RPA Recorder, and RPA Builder:

- Create and delete users.
- Edit the users' personal data.
- Assign privileges or roles to users that enable them to work in specific modules and, access RPA
  Builder and RPA Recorder, and assume responsibility for process projects.
- Assign a user to a process team.

## Before You Begin

- The tenant admin is created first and can create other users.
- If you are not a tenant admin, ask an administrator to
  [assign you the required privileges](#assign-privileges-to-a-user):

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Action</th><th>Required Privileges</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Open the <strong>User Management</strong> module.</p></td><td><p>User Management Open</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Create, edit, license, and delete users.</p></td><td><p>User Management Administration</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create a User

Create a user to give an employee access to RPA Manager, RPA Recorder, and RPA Builder. After
creation RPA Manager sends an email with a one-time password to the new user. The new user needs to
log in to RPA Manager and set a new password within the expiration time mentioned in the email.

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Create**.
3.  Complete the form **Create a New User**:
    - **Login Name**:

      Name for the user to log on to RPA Manager. After creation, the login name can no longer be
      changed.

    - **Time Zone**:

      Time zone the user works in. All times not marked otherwise are shown in the time zone of the
      user.

    - **Available/Assigned Roles/Privileges**:

      Click on a role or privilege to assign it to or remove it from the user.

4.  Click **Save**.

## Assign Privileges to a User

A user needs privileges to view and use the RPA Manager modules. This ensures that only authorized
users can access the modules. Assign privileges to a user when you create a new user or edit an
existing user.

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click either **Create** in the header bar to create a new user or **Edit**
    (![pen-to-paper symbol](_images/edit-icon.png)) in the panel of the user to edit.
3.  In the form **<\[Create,Edit\]> a \[New\] User**, scroll down to the selection list **Available
    Privileges**.
4.  Click a privilege to assign it to the user.
5.  Repeat the previous step until the user has all needed privileges.
6.  Click **Save**.

## Change Process Assignments of a User

Instead of editing a project and adding a user to its project team, you can change several project
assignments of a user at once:

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Assign Project** (![process-diagram symbol](_images/assignproject-icon.png)) in the
    panel of the user whose project assignments you want to change.

The view **Assign Process** shows you a list of all processes where the user is part of the process
team.

To change permissions for assigned projects to reflect changed user responsibilities:

1.  In the view **Assign Process**, for each process, select the checkboxes to permit the phases to
    the user.
2.  Click **Save**.

You cannot change permissions for the project manager, who always has permissions for every phase.

The table does not show advanced permissions, which are sometimes needed to transfer a process to
the next phase. To change these permissions,
[edit the process data sheet](processautomation-manage#edit-an-automation-project) in the **Process
Management** view of the **Process Automation** module.

To assign additional processes to a user to support the process team:

1.  In the view **Assign Process**, select a process from the dropdown **Select Process**.
2.  In the table **Process List**, in the row of the added process, select the checkboxes of all
    phases the user needs to work in.
3.  Click **Save**.

To remove a user from a process team:

1.  In the view **Assign process**, click **Delete** (![trash symbol](_images/delete-icon.png)) for
    each process to remove from the user.
2.  Click **Save**.

The project manager and the project owner cannot be deleted. To change them, use the function
[**New Process Owner**](processautomation-manage#change-the-process-owner-of-an-automation-project)
or
[**New Project Manager**](processautomation-manage#change-the-project-manager-of-an-automation-project)
in the **Process Management** view of the **Process Automation** module.

## Reset a User Password

Reset a password if the user forgot it. RPA Manager sends an email that contains a new temporary
password to the user. The user can then create a new password.

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Reset Password** (![key symbol](_images/resetpassword-icon.png)) in the panel of the
    user to edit.

## Change Your Password

Change your own password regularly. You are logged out in the process and have to log in again. The
password is used to log in to RPA Manager and RPA Builder.

1.  Click on your user name in the navigation side bar.
2.  Click **Profile** in the drop-down menu.
3.  In the **Profile** view, click **Change password**.
4.  Enter your old and new password.
5.  Click **OK**.
6.  Log in with your new password.

## Reset Your Password

If you forget your password or if your one-time password expires, click the link on RPA Manager
login page to reset it. You need to know your user name to reset your password.

1.  Navigate to the RPA Manager login screen.
2.  Click **Forgot your credentials?**.
3.  In the **Forgot your credentials** form, enter your username.
4.  Click **Continue**.

A confirmation request for creating a new password is emailed to you.

1.  Open the email in your email application.
2.  Click **Request New Credentials**.

A link to create a new password is emailed to you. The one-time password expires after 24 hours.

1.  Open the email in your email application.
2.  Copy or remember the one-time password.
3.  Click **Sign In**.
4.  In the RPA Manager login screen, enter your user name and the one-time password.

    > [!IMPORTANT] If you use two-factor authentication, append the time-based one-time password
    > (TOTP) to your one-time password (OTP) on login.
    >
    > For example, if your OTP is `Nz2tuQbtxpse?` and your TOTP password is `123456`, enter
    > `Nz2tuQbtxpse?123456` in the password field.

5.  Click **Sign In**.
6.  In the **Welcome to RPA Manager** form, enter a new password.
7.  Confirm and remember the password.
8.  Click **Reset Password**.

## Enable Two-Factor Authentication for Yourself

Enable two-factor authentication (2FA) for extra security.

When using two-factor authentication, you log in to the system with two mutually independent secret
factors.

The TOTP (Time-Based One-Time Password Algorithm) is set up in a special app on a mobile device.
Every thirty seconds, a new password is generated and this can be used for authentication within
this time period.

Both the computer on which RPA Manager is installed, and the mobile device on which the app for
generation of the one-time password is installed, must be able to determine the precise Unix time
for verification of the user. For this reason, the time on the devices used should be synchronized
with UTC (Universal Time Coordinated).

1.  Click on your user name in the navigation side bar.
2.  Click **Profile** in the drop-down menu.
3.  In the **Profile** view, click **Enable Two-Factor Authentication**.
4.  Follow the instructions in the window **Enable Two-Factor Authentication**.
5.  Click **Enable Two-Factor Authentication**.

Log in using your password and 2FA.

> [!IMPORTANT] Append the time-based one-time password (TOTP) directly to your password on login.
>
> For example, if your password is `My1stPassword!` and your TOTP is `123456`, enter
> `My1stPassword!123456` in the password field.

## Disable Two-Factor Authentication for Yourself

Two-factor authentication can also be deactivated for you by an administrator.

To disable two-factor authentication for yourself:

1.  Click on your user name in the navigation side bar.
2.  Click **Profile** in the drop-down menu.
3.  In the **Profile** view, click **Disable Two-Factor Authentication**.

Log in using only your password.

You can [enable 2FA](#enable2faforyourself) again.

## Deactivate Two-Factor Authentication for a User

To disable two-factor authentication for a user:

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Deactivate Two-Factor Authentication**
    (![user with open lock symbol](_images/userdeactivate2fa-icon.png)) in the panel of the user
    whose 2FA want to deactivate.
3.  Confirm the deactivation.

The user can [enable 2FA](#enable2faforyourself) again.

## Edit a User

Edit users to change their data or permissions:

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Edit** (![pen-to-paper symbol](_images/edit-icon.png)) in the panel of the user to edit.
3.  Edit the form **Edit a User**.

    For an explanation of the properties, see [**Create a new User**](#form-create-user).

4.  Click **Save**.

## Remove a User

Remove obsolete users. A user cannot be deleted if it is the owner or manager of a process. Deleted
users cannot be restored. Use the **Deactivate** function to remove a user only temporarily.

1.  Open the **Users** view of the **User Management** module.
2.  Click **Delete** (![trash symbol](_images/delete-icon.png)) in the panel of the user to edit.
3.  Confirm the removal.

## See Also

- [User Roles and Permissions](../rpa-home/automation-userrolesandpermissions)
---

# User Management Overview

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This topic is affected by the
[Anypoint Platform integration](../rpa-home/anypoint-migration-overview).

If you use an Anypoint account to login to RPA Manager, refer to the
[Anypoint Access Management documentation](https://docs.mulesoft.com/access-management/).

In the **User Management** module, you manage the users of RPA Manager and RPA Builder:

- Create and delete users.
- Edit a userâs personal data.
- Assign privileges or roles to a user that enable a user to work in specific modules.
- Create roles to combine privileges.
- License users for RPA Builder access.
- Mark a user as project manager.
- Assemble teams for task sharing.
- Mark teams as centers of excellence.
- Create user API keys to configure an API connector in MuleSoft Composer.
